summary: Retest of Contact.jsx onSubmit refactor and mobile-menu data-testids. ALL THREE requested items pass: (1) empty-form submit → red error toast 'Preenche nome, email e mensagem.' still works; (2) valid-payload submit while Web3Forms is CORS-blocked → warning toast 'Mensagem recebida e guardada. O envio por email ficou pendente.' shown, form cleared (all 4 inputs empty), and backend row persisted (GET /api/contact returns the new row with delivered=false); (3) mobile menu inner links all carry data-testid='nav-mobile-link-{work|about|services|experience|contact}'. The iteration_1 critical UX bug is resolved.
critical_code_review_comments: Contact.jsx onSubmit is now correctly structured: Web3Forms call and backend backup are independent try blocks; error toast only escalates when BOTH fail. Good.

(Pre-existing, not blocking) Web3Forms CORS is still rejected from the preview origin — server-side fallback now handles this gracefully so users always see warning + persisted backup. Whitelist preview/prod origins in the Web3Forms dashboard if direct email delivery is desired.
(Pre-existing) server.py contact endpoint swallows Mongo insert exceptions and still returns 200 (per spec). Recommend logger.exception so failures surface in logs.
seed_data_creation: Two new TEST_Retest_/TEST_Retest2_ messages inserted into contact_messages via the live form during this retest (delivered=false). Plus one TEST_RetestCurl via direct API probe. Safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Contact form fix verified end-to-end against live preview URL. Submit flow: Web3Forms fetch fails with CORS (expected), backend axios.post /api/contact succeeds, warning toast shown, form cleared, row persisted with delivered=false. Mobile menu testids confirmed. Backend pytest suite from iteration_1 (test_portfolio_api.py) was not re-run as backend code is unchanged and was already 10/10 green.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 1 RCA: single try/catch wrapping both Web3Forms fetch AND backend axios.post caused the backend backup to be skipped whenever the third-party fetch threw (CORS). Fix verified in iteration 2: Contact.jsx now has two independent try/catch blocks with local `delivered` and `stored` flags; toast logic is `(!delivered && !stored) → error; delivered → success; else → warning`, and setForm(initialForm) runs whenever at least one channel succeeded.
